summary: Validated 4 P1/P2 backlog items: (1) Universal 2-20 Player WS signaling — 56/56 regression_shield + 4/4 vibe_room_signaling tests pass, GET /api/vibe-room/{room}/peers returns {count:0,cap:20,peers:[]} live; (2) VibeRoomVoice mounts in Coliseum with vibe-room-voice + mic/video toggles + per-peer tile data-testids (clicks register, no errors); (3) HttpRoomShell wraps chess (theme=default) + connect4 (theme=matrix) with unified RoomMenuBar + Lobby/back; (4) Privy CSP trim works — 0 TypeErrors, 0 frame-ancestors CSP violations on /games preview URL; ?force_privy=1 mounts SDK as designed. Bonus: Dominoes /http-multiplayer-game/dominoes/x correctly redirects to /dominoes; mobile 390x844 Coliseum has no horizontal overflow and renders voice + qualifier center stage.

ui_bugs.issue: Buttons are clickable and produce no errors, but neither aria-pressed nor a data-active attribute changes between toggles, so a screen-reader user (and automated assertions) cannot detect on/off state. State change is only conveyed via icon-swap.

action_items: OPTIONAL (LOW): add aria-pressed (or data-active) toggling on vibe-room-mic-toggle / vibe-room-video-toggle so screen readers + Playwright assertions can verify on/off state.

DOC NIT: review request listed /play/chess + /play/connect4 + /play/dominoes as the test paths, but the actual mounted routes are /http-multiplayer-game/<gameType>/<roomId>. Updating the README / test matrix to use the canonical path will avoid future confusion.

FOLLOW-UP (already noted by main agent): VibeRoomVoice currently renders placeholder letter-avatar tiles — actual WebRTC media negotiation is intentionally deferred. Signaling itself is real and tested.
critical_code_review_comments: vibe_room_signaling.py is clean (~180 lines, async-safe locks, broadcast fan-out tolerates send failures, hard cap = 20). Defensive 'replace stale ws under same user_id on refresh' is a nice touch.
Coliseum e2e probe correctly skips when Chromium is missing — good CI hygiene.
PrivyAuthProvider PRIVY_SKIP_PATTERNS approach is sound; the ?force_privy=1 override gives QA a clean path to test Privy on preview without flipping env vars.
test_credentials: POST /api/auth/demo-login (no body) → fresh demo_<id> user with token + 5000 credits.
seed_data_creation: Coliseum tables created on-the-fly via /api/vibe654/tournament/create-table (host=demo user, buy-in 100, max_players 4).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Universal 2-20 Player signaling endpoint mounted at /api/vibe-room/ws/{room_id}/{user_id} (NOT /ws/vibe/...). HttpRoomShell wraps every render of /http-multiplayer-game/<gameType>/<roomId>. /play/<game> paths from the review request do NOT exist as routes — use the http-multiplayer-game canonical path. Privy is intentionally skipped on *.preview.emergentagent.com; ?force_privy=1 re-enables it for QA.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failing tests in this iteration.
